skill.name: video-enhancement
skill.description: AI Video Enhancement - Upscale video resolution, improve quality, denoise, sharpen, enhance low-quality videos to HD/4K. Supports local video files, remote URLs (YouTube, Bilibili), auto-download, real-time progress tracking.
skill.summary: AI-powered video upscaling and quality enhancement tool for transforming low-resolution videos into HD/4K content with denoising and sharpening capabilities.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate video enhancement skill that integrates with the verging.ai API service. Static analysis flagged 82 patterns, but most are false positives: hardcoded URLs are documented public API endpoints, API key references are for user-provided authentication (not secret exfiltration), and shell commands in documentation are usage examples for ffmpeg/yt-dlp/curl. The skill requires network access for API calls, environment variable for user API key, external tools for video processing, and temp directory for file handling. These are expected behaviors for a video processing tool. Risk level is LOW due to external service dependency and third-party API usage.

low_findings.title: External API Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill depends on third-party verging.ai API service for video enhancement processing. All video processing occurs on external servers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill explicitly requires verging.ai API service for core functionality - videos are uploaded to external R2 storage for AI processing.

low_findings.title: User API Key Required
low_findings.description: Skill requires users to provide their own verging.ai API key via environment variable. Users must trust the third-party service with their credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: VERGING_API_KEY is required environment variable - documented usage pattern but users should be aware of credential sharing with third party.

low_findings.title: External Tool Dependencies
low_findings.description: Skill requires external binaries (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, curl) for video download and processing. These tools must be installed on the user's system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill explicitly requires yt-dlp for remote video downloads and ffmpeg/ffprobe for video processing - standard video tools but external dependencies.

low_findings.title: Remote Video Download
low_findings.description: Skill can download videos from remote URLs including YouTube and Bilibili. Users should ensure they have rights to process downloaded content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: yt-dlp integration allows downloading from YouTube/Bilibili - legitimate functionality but users should be aware of copyright implications.

content.user_title: Enhance videos with AI upscaling and quality improvement
content.value_statement: Transform low-quality videos into HD or 4K resolution using AI-powered enhancement. This skill handles denoising, sharpening, and resolution upscaling through the verging.ai API service with support for local files and remote URLs.
actual_capabilities: Upscale video resolution from low quality to HD or 4K using AI
Denoise and sharpen low-quality video footage
Process local video files in MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, and WebM formats
Download and process videos from remote URLs including YouTube and Bilibili
Trim video segments using start and end time parameters
Track job progress with real-time status updates
limitations: Maximum video duration is 30 seconds for processing
Maximum file size is 500MB per video
Requires verging.ai API key and internet connection
Requires external tools (yt-dlp, ffmpeg) installed for remote URL and trimming features

use_cases.title: Restore old family videos
use_cases.description: Enhance old or degraded footage to modern HD quality for archival and sharing purposes.

use_cases.title: Improve content creation quality
use_cases.description: Upscale lower-resolution source footage before publishing to social media or streaming platforms.

use_cases.title: Enhance surveillance footage
use_cases.description: Improve clarity of security camera recordings for better visibility and identification.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video enhancement
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance this video file using normal quality settings: /video-enhancement -v ./my-video.mp4
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick enhancement of a local video file with default settings

prompt_templates.title: HD quality enhancement
prompt_templates.prompt: Upscale this video to the highest quality: /video-enhancement -v ./old-footage.mp4 --hd
prompt_templates.scenario: Maximum quality enhancement for important videos requiring HD output

prompt_templates.title: Process specific video segment
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance only the segment from 10 to 25 seconds: /video-enhancement -v ./long-video.mp4 --start 10 --end 25
prompt_templates.scenario: Enhancing a specific portion of a longer video to save processing time and credits

prompt_templates.title: Remote URL with download
prompt_templates.prompt: Download and enhance this YouTube video, then save the result locally: /video-enhancement -v "https://youtube.com/watch?v=xxx" --hd --download
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing a remote video URL and automatically downloading the enhanced result

output_examples.output: Video uploaded successfully. Processing started. Your video will be enhanced from 480p to 1080p HD resolution. Estimated processing time: 2-3 minutes. Job ID: job_abc123. Progress: 0% - Uploading video... Progress: 100% - Upload complete. Progress: 45% - AI enhancement in progress. Enhancement complete! Download your HD video at: https://img.panpan8.com/video-enhancement/2026-03-18/enhanced-family-reunion.mp4

output_examples.input: Enhance the YouTube video from 5 to 15 seconds with HD mode
output_examples.output: Downloading video from YouTube using yt-dlp. Video downloaded: 30 seconds duration. Trimming video from 5s to 15s (10 second segment). Checking your credits balance: 50 credits available. HD mode requires 30 credits (10 sec x 3 credits/sec). Credits sufficient. Uploading trimmed video for HD enhancement. Job created successfully. Processing progress: 0% -> 25% -> 60% -> 100%. Your HD enhanced video is ready at: https://img.panpan8.com/video-enhancement/2026-03-18/hd-clip-xyz789.mp4

best_practices: Trim videos to only the essential segments before enhancement to reduce processing time and credit consumption
Use HD mode only when the highest quality output is required, as it consumes 3x more credits
Ensure you have the legal right to process any videos, especially when downloading from remote sources like YouTube
anti_patterns: Do not process videos longer than 30 seconds - the API will reject them and you will lose credits
Avoid uploading videos containing copyrighted content you do not own or have permission to modify
Do not share your VERGING_API_KEY in code repositories or public communications

faq.question: How do I get a verging.ai API key?
faq.answer: Visit https://verging.ai, create an account or log in, click your avatar in the top right corner, select 'API Keys', and create a new key. Set it as an environment variable using: export VERGING_API_KEY="your_key"

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, and WebM formats. Videos must be under 500MB and no longer than 30 seconds in duration.

faq.question: How many credits does video enhancement cost?
faq.answer: Normal mode costs 1 credit per second of video. HD mode costs 3 credits per second. A 10-second video in normal mode costs 10 credits; in HD mode it costs 30 credits.

faq.question: Can I enhance videos from YouTube or Bilibili?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can download videos from YouTube, Bilibili, and other supported sites using yt-dlp. You must have the legal right to process the downloaded content.

faq.question: What tools do I need installed?
faq.answer: For local video files: no additional tools needed. For remote URLs: yt-dlp is required. For trimming videos: ffmpeg and ffprobe are required. The skill can help install yt-dlp if missing.

faq.question: Where are my processed videos stored?
faq.answer: Videos are processed on verging.ai servers and returned via a public URL. Temporary local files are stored in /tmp/verging-video-enhancement/ and automatically cleaned up after processing.
